county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Carrollton holds records from 1821. Greene County is in the Illinois River valley — early settlers came from Virginia, Kentucky, and Tennessee. The Bluffs area along the Illinois River attracted early farmers. Greene County Historical and Genealogical Society. Carrollton was an early Illinois courthouse town. Check Madison County for pre-1821 records. Strong Methodist and Baptist church records from early settlement. IRAD at University of Illinois Springfield. Prairie farming and livestock records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1821. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1821. County Clerk issues licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (details improve post-1870s). Statewide marriage registration from 1962.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1821. Illinois Circuit Court handles probate matters. Estate inventories often list heirs and personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalizations (pre-1906), and judgments from 1821. Illinois Circuit Court is the trial court of general jurisdiction. Early records include debt cases, divorce proceedings, and guardianship matters that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Greene County Clerk, Carrollton / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax assessment rolls list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. County Clerk records from 1821. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Illinois State Archives holds some county tax records on microfilm.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax records at Illinois State Archives on microfilm; IRAD holdings vary by county

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Greene County
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Morris holds records from 1841. Illinois & Michigan Canal passed through Grundy County — canal construction worker records and canal-town records in Morris and Seneca document Irish and German laborers. Coal mining developed in the 1860s. Grundy County Historical Society in Morris. Check LaSalle County for pre-1841 records. IRAD at Illinois State University. Dresden Nuclear Power Station area. Morris is on the Illinois River. Strong Catholic church records from Irish canal workers.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1841. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1841. County Clerk issues licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (details improve post-1870s). Statewide marriage registration from 1962.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1841. Illinois Circuit Court handles probate matters. Estate inventories often list heirs and personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

source_profiles.repository: Grundy County Circuit Clerk, Morris / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court. Post-1906 copies at NARA Great Lakes Region (Chicago). Illinois attracted massive European immigration — German, Irish, Swedish, Norwegian, Czech, Polish, Italian, Lithuanian, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Illinois naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

source_profiles.source_name: Illinois & Michigan Canal Records — Grundy County
source_profiles.repository: Illinois State Archives / I&M Canal National Heritage Corridor
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Canal construction records, land sales, and worker rolls. The I&M Canal (1836-1848) connected Lake Michigan to the Illinois River. Canal construction employed thousands of Irish immigrant laborers. Canal land grant records document property sales along the corridor.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Canal Commission records at Illinois State Archives

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in McLeansboro holds records from 1821. Hamilton County is in southern Illinois — early settlers came from Upland South (Kentucky, Tennessee, North Carolina). McLeansboro was a modest county seat town. Oil production in the 20th century generated land lease records. Hamilton County Historical Society. Check White County for pre-1821 records. IRAD at Southern Illinois University Carbondale. Baptist and Methodist church records are the most important denominational sources. Township records supplement county records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1821. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.

source_profiles.source_name: Oil and Gas Lease Records — Hamilton County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease agreements recorded in county deed books. Illinois oil fields (particularly in southeastern Illinois) generated extensive lease and mineral rights records from the early 1900s onward. These records identify landowners and track property transactions.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved in county deed records

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Carthage holds records from 1825. CRITICAL for Mormon/LDS research: Nauvoo in Hancock County was the major Mormon settlement (1839-1846). Joseph Smith was assassinated at Carthage Jail in 1844. Nauvoo temple records, city records, and church histories are essential — many at LDS Church History Library in Salt Lake City. After the Mormon exodus, Icarian French utopian community settled in Nauvoo (1849). German Catholic and German Evangelical settlers. Hancock County Historical Society. IRAD at Western Illinois University.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1825. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax assessment rolls list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. County Clerk records from 1825. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Illinois State Archives holds some county tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Hancock County
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

source_profiles.source_name: Military Tract Bounty Land Records — Hancock County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: The Military Tract between the Illinois and Mississippi Rivers was reserved for War of 1812 veterans' bounty land warrants. Many warrants were sold to speculators. Patent and warrant records identify original claimants and purchasers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-documented — patents at BLM-GLO; warrant records at NARA

source_profiles.repository: Hancock County Circuit Clerk, Carthage / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court. Post-1906 copies at NARA Great Lakes Region (Chicago). Illinois attracted massive European immigration — German, Irish, Swedish, Norwegian, Czech, Polish, Italian, Lithuanian, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Illinois naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

source_profiles.source_name: Nauvoo LDS Church Records — Hancock County
source_profiles.repository: LDS Church History Library, Salt Lake City / Community of Christ Library-Archives, Independence MO
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Nauvoo was the largest Mormon settlement (1839-1846) with over 10,000 residents. Church records include blessings, baptisms, endowments, and sealing records. Nauvoo city records, Nauvoo Legion muster rolls, and temple records. Joseph Smith's assassination (1844) and the 1846 exodus are heavily documented.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Extensive records at LDS Church History Library; FamilySearch has indexed collections

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Elizabethtown holds records from 1839. Hardin is Illinois' smallest county by population and one of the most rugged — Shawnee National Forest covers much of the county. Ohio River community with connections to Kentucky families. Cave-in-Rock was a notorious outlaw hideout in the early 1800s. Check Pope County for pre-1839 records. Hardin County Historical and Genealogical Society. IRAD at Southern Illinois University Carbondale. Fluorspar mining was a major industry — mine records document workers.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1839. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Oquawka holds records from 1841. Henderson County is on the Mississippi River across from Burlington, Iowa — check Des Moines County, Iowa for related families. Oquawka is one of Illinois' smallest county seats. Check Warren County for pre-1841 records. Henderson County Historical Society. Strong connections to Des Moines County, Iowa families. IRAD at Western Illinois University. Early settlers from New England, New York, and Upland South. Mississippi River ferry and steamboat records.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Cambridge holds records from 1825. Henry County attracted Swedish immigrants in the 1840s-1850s — Bishop Hill was a famous Swedish communal settlement (1846-1861). Bishop Hill Colony records are at the Bishop Hill Heritage Association and Knox College Archives. Check Fulton County for pre-1825 records. Henry County Historical Society. Kewanee was an important industrial and railroad town. IRAD at Western Illinois University. Strong Augustana Lutheran church records from Swedish settlers.

source_profiles.source_name: Swedish American Church Records — Henry County
source_profiles.repository: Swenson Swedish Immigration Research Center, Rock Island / Augustana Heritage Association
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Augustana Lutheran and other Swedish denomination church records — baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Bishop Hill Colony records (Henry County). Swenson Center at Augustana College holds the largest Swedish American research collection in the U.S.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Swenson Center has extensive microfilm and original records; many indexed

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Watseka holds records from 1833. Iroquois County is in eastern Illinois on the Indiana border — check Newton County, Indiana for cross-border families. Named for the Iroquois River. Sugar Creek area was an early settlement. Iroquois County Historical Society in Watseka. Check Vermilion County for pre-1833 records. IRAD at the University of Illinois. French-Canadian fur trade heritage — early place names reflect this. Prairie farming heritage with strong agricultural census records.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Murphysboro holds records from 1816. Southern Illinois University at Carbondale (1869) holds IRAD collections for southern Illinois counties. Jackson County Historical Society. Coal mining heritage — Murphysboro and surrounding areas had significant mining operations. Murphysboro was struck by the devastating Tri-State Tornado (1925) which killed 234 in Murphysboro alone — some local records were destroyed. Check Randolph County for pre-1816 records. French colonial connections to Kaskaskia.

source_profiles.repository: Illinois State Archives / Illinois Department of Mines and Minerals
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Mine inspection reports, employment records, accident reports, and company records. Illinois coal mining drew workers from Italy, Lithuania, Poland, and other European countries as well as Appalachian migrants. Annual coal reports published by the State of Illinois list mines and operators.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Mine inspection records at Illinois State Archives; some company records in local collections

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Newton holds records from 1831. Created from Clay and Crawford — check both for pre-1831 records. Jasper County is a rural agricultural county in southeastern Illinois. Newton was a small but active county seat. Oil production in the 20th century generated land records. Jasper County Historical and Genealogical Society. IRAD at Eastern Illinois University. Early settlers from Kentucky and Tennessee. Strong Baptist and Methodist church records. Cumberland Presbyterian records are also important.
